full_text:
Hyderabad: Heatwave alert was issued for several districts in Telangana after the Meteorological Department forecast dry weather and severe hot wind conditions across parts of the state on Monday.
Officials issued a yellow alert for districts including Rajanna Sircilla, Asifabad, Adilabad, Mancherial, Nirmal, Nizamabad and Jagtial. The department advised residents to remain cautious during peak daytime temperatures and avoid unnecessary outdoor activity.
Weather officials said high temperatures and dry conditions were likely to continue in northern Telangana districts over the next few days. Authorities also warned that hot winds could intensify in some areas during afternoon hours.
Meanwhile, the Amaravati Meteorological Department predicted light rainfall and thunderstorms in parts of Andhra Pradesh. Officials said isolated showers could occur in Manyam, Alluri, Polavaram, Chittoor, Anantapur and Kurnool districts.
The department stated that weather conditions remained unstable in several north coastal districts of Andhra Pradesh. However, intense heat conditions were expected to continue in multiple mandals despite the possibility of scattered rainfall.
Heatwave alert continues in north coastal areas
Officials warned of severe heat conditions in several mandals of Srikakulam district, including Amadalavalasa, Burja, Hiramandalam, Kanchili, Nandigam, Pathapatnam and Sompeta.
Authorities also forecast high temperatures in parts of Vizianagaram district. The affected mandals include Badangi, Bobbili, Bondapalli, Dattirajeru, Gantyada, Merakamudidam, Ramabhadrapuram, Santakaviti, Terlam and Vangara.
In Manyam district, weather officials issued warnings for Balijipeta, Garugubilli, Gumma Lakshmipuram, Jiyyammavalasa, Komarada, Kurupam, Pachipenta, Palakonda, Salur, Seethampeta and Veeraghattam mandals.
Officials advised people to stay hydrated and take precautions against prolonged exposure to the sun. They also urged elderly people, children and outdoor workers to remain alert during extreme heat conditions.

full_text:

Telangana heatwave intensifies as temperatures soar up to 44 degrees Celsius across districts
Telangana continues to reel under a severe heatwave with temperatures ranging between 41°C and 44°C across several districts. Adilabad recorded the highest at 44.3°C. IMD data shows rising night temperatures and widespread discomfort across northern, western and urban regions.
Hyderabad: With the summer season peaking, almost all the districts in the State have continued to remain at the receiving end of a punishing heatwave, which has tightened its grip across Telangana.
In the last 24-hours, between Sunday and 8.30 am on Monday, according to India Meteorological Department (IMD) Hyderabad weather data, multiple districts have recorded maximum temperatures ranging between 41 degrees Celsius and 44 degrees Celsius.
The relentless dry spell, marked by clear skies and zero rainfall, is also not providing the public much relief at night. The nighttime temperatures during night have remained at 3.5 degrees Celsius above normal,making it very difficult for people, who continue to remain suffocated under the heat stress.
In terms of peak regional impact, the northern and western districts of Telangana are bearing the absolute brunt of this severe spell, with maximum temperatures consistently breaching the 41 degrees Celsius mark.
Adilabad emerged as the hottest district in the state, recording a maximum temperature of 44.3 degrees Celsius, closely followed by Nizamabad (43.6), Medak (42.6), Ramagundam (41.2) and Nalgonda district recording 41 degrees Celsius.
Within the Hyderabad metropolitan area, the distribution of heat showed variations across different regions, with a few urban stations pushing past the 40 degrees Celsius threshold.
The highest daytime temperature in the city was recorded at Rajendranagar, which hit 40.1 degrees Celsius followed by Hakimpet (39.8), Patancheru (39.2) and Begumpet recorded a maximum of 39.1 degrees Celsius.

Heatwave warning for Telangana for next seven days
The India Meteorological Department has issued a seven-day heatwave warning for parts of Telangana, with temperatures likely to cross 45 degrees Celsius in several districts. The state government has issued public health advisories and strengthened medical preparedness across healthcare facilities
Hyderabad: The India Meteorological Department (IMD) has issued a heatwave warning for the next seven days in parts of Telangana.
The Hyderabad Meteorological Centre has forecast a gradual rise in the maximum temperature by 2-3 degrees Celsius during the next three days in Telangana.
Some districts are likely to see maximum temperatures crossing 45 degrees Celsius between May 19 and May 24.
Heatwave conditions are very likely to prevail at isolated pockets in Adilabad, Komuram Bheem Asifabad, Mancherial, Nirmal, Nizamabad, Jagtial, and Rajanna Sircilla districts on May 18. The maximum temperature in these districts may cross 45 degrees Celsius.
Parts of Telangana experienced heatwave conditions on Sunday, too.
According to the Telangana Development Planning Society, the highest temperature of 45.7 degrees Celsius was recorded in Bheemgal mandal in Nizamabad district. Mosra mandal in the same district recorded 45.5 degrees Celsius.
Heatwave conditions also prevailed in a couple of mandals of Nirmal district. Dasturabad recorded a maximum temperature of 45.3 while Tanur experienced 45.1 degrees Celsius.
Kollur in Banswada mandal of Kamareddy district saw a maximum temperature of 45.2 degrees Celsius.
Sixteen out of 33 districts in the state recorded a maximum temperature of 42 degrees Celsius or above.
The average maximum temperature in the state was 40.8 degrees Celsius.
In the Greater Hyderabad Municipal Corporation (GHMC) limits, the average maximum temperature was 40.4 degrees Celsius. The highest temperature of 42.3 degrees Celsius was recorded in the Goshamahal circle.
In view of the heat wave warning issued by the IMD, the Director of Public Health and Family Welfare, Dr B. Ravinder Nayak. has issued an advisory for protection against heat waves, listing dos and don’ts for the citizens.
As a part of precautionary measures, the government has made elaborate arrangements by providing the special beds, I.V fluids, and essential medicines at all the public health facilities and ORS sachets are made available with ANMs/ASHAs/Anganwadi workers to meet any exigencies.

full_text:
Heatwave sweeps across Telangana, mercury crosses 45°C; IMD issues 7-day alert
The Hyderabad Meteorological Centre has forecast a 2-3°C rise in maximum temperatures over the next three days, with heatwave conditions expected to intensify between May 19 and 24
By Newsmeter NetworkRepresentational Image
Hyderabad: India Meteorological Department (IMD) has issued a seven-day heatwave alert in Telangana.
Mercury has crossed 45 degrees Celsius in several districts. IMD has warned that temperatures could remain above 45 degrees Celsius in Telangana in the days to come.
The warning has raised concerns over public health and heat-related illnesses as the state prepares for a prolonged spell of scorching weather.
The Hyderabad Meteorological Centre has forecast a gradual rise of 2-3 degrees Celsius in maximum temperatures during the next three days, indicating that heatwave conditions are likely to intensify further between May 19 and May 24.
North Telangana districts likely to bear the brunt
According to the IMD, isolated pockets in Adilabad, Komuram Bheem Asifabad, Mancherial, Nirmal, Nizamabad, Jagtial, and Rajanna Sircilla districts are likely to experience severe heatwave conditions on May 18.
Officials warned that temperatures in several of these districts may exceed 45 degrees Celsius, especially during afternoon hours.
Nizamabad records highest temperature
Several districts in north Telangana already witnessed intense heat on Sunday.
Data from the Telangana Development Planning Society revealed that Bheemgal mandal in Nizamabad district recorded the state’s highest temperature at 45.7 degrees Celsius. Mosra mandal in the same district registered 45.5 degrees Celsius.
In Nirmal district, Dasturabad touched 45.3 degrees Celsius while Tanur recorded 45.1 degrees Celsius. Kollur village in Banswada mandal of Kamareddy district also experienced severe heat, with the mercury climbing to 45.2 degrees Celsius.
Half the State crosses 42°C
The heatwave conditions spread across large parts of Telangana, with 16 out of the 33 districts recording maximum temperatures of 42 degrees Celsius or above.
The average maximum temperature across the state stood at 40.8 degrees Celsius.
Hyderabad too witnessed rising temperatures, with the average maximum temperature within Greater Hyderabad Municipal Corporation (GHMC) limits recorded at 40.4 degrees Celsius. Goshamahal circle emerged as the hottest location in the city at 42.3 degrees Celsius.
Health department issues Public Advisory
In view of the worsening heat conditions, Director of Public Health and Family Welfare Dr B. Ravinder Nayak issued an advisory urging citizens to take precautions against heat-related illnesses.
People have been advised to avoid stepping out during peak afternoon hours, drink plenty of water, wear loose cotton clothing, and seek immediate medical assistance if symptoms such as dizziness, dehydration, fatigue, or heatstroke occur.
Government readies hospitals for emergencies
The Telangana government has also stepped up preparedness measures across public health facilities.
Authorities said special beds, intravenous fluids, and essential medicines have been arranged at government hospitals to handle heatwave-related emergencies.
ORS sachets have also been distributed through ANMs, ASHA workers, and Anganwadi staff to provide immediate relief in vulnerable areas.
Officials urged the public, especially children, elderly persons, and outdoor workers, to remain cautious as temperatures are expected to rise further in the coming days.

title: IMD forecasts severe heatwave in Telangana - The Hans India
search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo OR "hot loo winds" OR "intense heat" OR "unbearable heat" OR "deadly heat" OR heat OR "weather forecast" OR "weather update" OR "weather alert" OR "IMD forecast" OR "IMD prediction" OR "IMD alert" OR IMD OR "rising temperatures" OR "temperatures expected to rise" OR "relief from heat" OR "respite from heat" OR "heat respite" OR "respite from scorching heat") Telangana
full_text:
Hyderabad: The Indian Meteorological Department (IMD), Hyderabad Centre has predicted severe heat wave conditions in parts of the state during May 17-22, with temperatures likely to touch 44 degrees Celsius. Hence, the Met department has issued a red alert for a few districts.
Consequently, the Health Department has issued an advisory to the people with ‘dos and don’ts’, asking them not to venture out unless it is urgent. In its forecast issued on Sunday, the IMD said that there will be a gradual rise in maximum temperatures by 2 to 3°C over the next three days in the state. The IMD issued a warning that heatwave conditions are very likely to prevail at isolated pockets in Adilabad, Komaram Bheem Asifabad, Mancherial, Nirmal and Jagtial districts of Telangana.
Further, the IMD warned, thunderstorm accompanied with lightning and gusty winds (40-50 kmph) are likely to occur at isolated places in Mulugu, Bhadradri Kothagudem, Khammam, Nalgonda, Suryapet, Vikarabad, Sangareddy, Mahabubnagar, Nagarkurnool, Wanaparthy, Narayanpet and Jogulamba Gadwal districts of Telangana.
The IMD issued a warning that from May 20-22, heatwave conditions are very likely to prevail at isolated pockets in Adilabad, Komuram Bheem Asifabad, Mancherial, Nirmal, Nizamabad, Jagtial, Rajanna Sircilla, Karimnagar, Peddapalli, Khammam, Suryapet, Jayashankar Bhupalpally, Khammam, Suryapet and Mahabubabad districts of Telangana. A red alert has been issued by the IMD for Adilabad, Jatial, Komuram Bheem, Mancherial, Nirmal, Peddapalli, Rajanna Sircilla and other districts. The temperatures in these districts are likely to be between 41°C and 44°C till May 21. Meanwhile, the Health Department has issued an advisory to the people of the state to take precautions.
Under dos, the department has asked people to stay hydrated, drink sufficient water as and when possible, even if you are not thirsty. Use oral rehydration solution (ORS) and consume homemade drinks like lemon water, buttermilk / fruit juices / (homemade ORS: Mix one litre of drinking water, six level teaspoons of sugar and half level teaspoon of salt, stir well until all components are dissolved). Carry water during the travelling time, eat seasonal fruits and vegetables with high water content like watermelon, muskmelon, oranges, grapes, pineapples, cucumbers or any other locally available fruits and vegetables.
Officials have advised citizens to wear thin loose cotton garments preferably light coloured ones. Cover your head by using umbrella / hat / cap/ towel or any other traditional headgears during exposure to the direct sunlight.
Under the don’ts, officials advised people to avoid going out in the sun, especially between 12 noon and 3 pm., avoid strenuous activities in the afternoon. Do not go out barefoot, avoid cooking during the peak hours, avoid consuming alcohol, tea, coffee and carbonated soft drinks or drinks with large amount of sugar as these actually lead to loss of more body-fluid or may cause stomach cramps, avoid high-protein, salty, spicy and oily food. Do not eat stale food.
The heat wave danger signs include nausea and vomiting, hot, red and dry skin, body temperature ≥40 0C or 104 0F, throbbing headache, anxiety, dizziness and fainting, muscle weakness or cramps, rapid heartbeat, rapid, shallow breathing, altered mental sensorium with disorientation: confusion, agitation, irritability, ataxia, seizure, and coma.
Officials have advised seeking immediate medical attention from the nearby government health facilities if any of the above features are observed.
The Director of Public Health Dr B Ravinder Naik said that as a part of precautionary measures, the government has made elaborate arrangements by providing the Special beds, I.V fluids, essential medicines at all the public health facilities and ORS sachets are made available with ANMs/ASHAs/Anganwadi workers to meet any exigencies.

India Meteorological Department issues orange alert for Telangana heat wave
Heads up, Telangana, IMD has issued an orange alert for a severe heat wave sticking around until May 20.
Temperatures are set to climb by two to three degrees Celsius, with highs between 41 and 44 degrees Celsius, especially hitting northern districts like Adilabad, Karimnagar, and Mancherial.
Telangana health officials urge hydration
With the mercury rising, there's a bigger risk of dehydration and heat stroke: kids, older folks, and anyone with health conditions should be extra careful.
Health officials suggest skipping outdoor activities during peak afternoon hours, wearing light cotton clothes, and sipping on ORS or lemon water to stay hydrated.
Government hospitals and health centers are ready with IV fluids, medicines, and ORS packets if things get tough.

title: Mercury set to soar as heatwave likely returns in Telangana - Dailyhunt
full_text:
Hyderabad: Telangana is bracing for a renewed heatwave, as the India Meteorological Department (IMD), Hyderabad forecasts a significant temperature surge across the state starting this week.
While scattered thunderstorms provided a temporary break, the IMD's Sunday evening weather forecast warns of a 'gradual rise' in maximum temperatures by 2 degree Celsius to 3 degree Celsius over the next three to four days.
Isolated light to moderate rain and thunderstorms, accompanied by gusty winds of 30-40 kmph, will likely persist on Monday in districts including Adilabad, Hyderabad, Khammam, and Nalgonda. However, by Tuesday, dry weather is expected to prevail across the entire state, effectively ending the cooling effect of the recent rain.
The northern districts including Adilabad, Nirmal, and Nizamabad are expected to feel the brunt of the mercury, which is expected to range between 41 degree Celsius and 44 degree Celsius.
By the middle of the coming week, the high-temperature 'orange alert' zone is expected to expand southward and westward. Major centers including Hyderabad, Rangareddy, Vikarabad, and Mahabubnagar are projected to transition from the present 36 to 40 degree Celsius to the higher 41 degree Celsius and 44 degree Celsius range by Wednesday.
Meanwhile, eastern districts like Bhadradri Kothagudem and Mulugu are forecast to stay slightly cooler, maintaining temperatures between 36 and 40.
In the last 24 hours (till 8:30 am on Sunday), Adilabad recorded the state’s highest temperature at 40.5 degree Celsius (1.6°C below normal), followed closely by Nizamabad at 40.4 while Khammam and Nalgonda both reached the 40 degree Celsius.
In the capital, the GHMC area continued to witness a relatively low 36.4 degree Celsius at Begumpet, while Dundigal recorded 37.6 and Medak witnessed 38 degree Celsius. As the dry spell takes hold on Tuesday, the maximum temperatures in Greater Hyderabad are expected to climb rapidly and range between 41 and 44 degree Celsius.

title: Heatwave Conditions Intensify Across Several Telangana Districts - The South India Times
full_text:
- Heatwave conditions increase in Telangana districts
The intensity of summer heat has increased rapidly across Telangana, forcing weather officials to issue warnings for several districts expected to experience strong heatwave conditions in the coming days. Authorities said dry weather is likely to continue across most parts of the state, especially in northern Telangana regions where temperatures are climbing steadily.
Districts including Adilabad, Nizamabad, and Jagtial are expected to face severe hot winds during afternoon hours. Similar weather conditions are likely in Sirisilla, Nirmal, and Mancherial districts, prompting the Meteorological Department to issue a yellow alert for these areas. Officials advised people to avoid unnecessary travel during peak daytime hours and remain cautious while stepping outdoors.
Health experts have also warned that children and elderly people are more vulnerable during extreme heat conditions. Residents have been asked to stay hydrated, wear protective clothing, and avoid direct exposure to sunlight for long periods. Authorities stressed that taking preventive measures can help reduce heat related health problems during the ongoing weather conditions.
While Telangana continues to experience rising temperatures, parts of Andhra Pradesh may witness a different weather pattern over the next few days. Light rainfall is expected in districts such as Manyam, Alluri, and Kurnool, offering temporary relief from the intense summer heat.
Weather officials also warned about the possibility of thunderstorms and lightning in some areas. Farmers and people working in open fields were advised to remain alert during changing weather conditions. Scattered showers are also likely in parts of Anantapur and Chittoor districts, bringing slightly cooler temperatures to those regions.
